content.body: ljharb: `x = new Promise(()=>{}); Promise.try(() => x) === x // false` was this discussed? seems like it would be nice if it were `true`, like if you'd done a try-catch with a `Promise.resolve()` in the non-throwy case, to avoid the extra allocation/microtask
content.formatted_body: ljharb: x = new Promise(()=>{}); Promise.try(() => x) === x // false was this discussed? seems like it would be nice if it were true, like if you'd done a try-catch with a Promise.resolve() in the non-throwy case, to avoid the extra allocation/microtask

content.body: it wasn't discussed afaik, and i'd be fine with tightening that if possible
since Promise.resolve offers that semantic it's kind of silly not to use it anywhere else we can
would clean up some code I'm writing, at least
I bet we could get away with changing that still, it's relatively new
